properties.Ecological/Environmental: Arqueological findings indicate this could too have been Xapawiyemetá
Located on the Santiago River in what is now the Metropolitan Region of Guadalajara, a site of major biodiversity that is today amongst the most polluted rivers in the hemisphere
properties.Cultural Elements: Early tourist destination for Guadalajara residents. A site of picnics and marriage proposals
properties.Socio-Political: Site of major industrial production after 1965 economic trade agreements that pushed siting industry in this region. Silicon Valley, textiles, assembly plants

properties.Ecological/Environmental: The largest lake in Mexico, Chapala is shaped by the convergence of the Lerma-Santiago river system, linking central and western Mexico, and occupying the shorelines of both Jalisco and Michoacán. Heavily influenced by agricultural and fishing communities of Nawatl, Coca, and Mestizo backgrounds. Decades of industrial discharge into the riversystem have brought about recurring ecological crises for Chapala that range from plagues of algae to high levels of cancer, kidney and skin diseases amongst the local populations
properties.Cultural Elements: Xapawiyemetá, the Waters of the South, is one of the principal Wixarika sacred pilgrimage sites and the place where the great flood was believed to have been centered
properties.Socio-Political: Real estate encroachment, land use change, agroindustry, violence

properties.Cultural Elements: One of the five key sacred pilgrimage places in Wixarika tradition

properties.Ecological/Environmental: Declared an oasis of the highplateaus of San Luis Potosí, part of the natural and cultural region

properties.Socio-Political: Recently gained its autonomous stature after years of separation from Tateikié and being labeled an
. Surrounded by Mestizo and O'dam communities

properties.Other names: El Colorín, Colorado de la Mora, Salvador Allende (two Wixarika towns along periphery)
properties.Ecological/Environmental: One of several dams built along Santiago River since the 1980s. El Cajón Dam is just a few kilometers downriver. Located in a key area ecologically where there are vast amounts of fresh water, mountains with biodiversity.
properties.Socio-Political: The dam displaced several Wixarika communities (many of these individuals would become residents of Colonia Zitakua in Tepic) and was built during the controversial Salinas de Gortari presidency

properties.Ecological/Environmental: A rare volcanic remnant in an area otherwise without volcanic archaeology. Was at one point ocean; there are shell remnants in the Sierra de Catorce.
properties.Cultural Elements: Important site of pilgrimage for the community of Tateikié and other Wixárika communities; a popular destination for new agers

properties.Ecological/Environmental: Traverses central Mexico up and through the US-Mx border
Sierra of Catorce, site of colonial mining, forests continue to struggle to grow
properties.Cultural Elements: Site where the sun was born after a boy sacrificed himself
properties.Socio-Political: Site of continued contested land use and First Majestic Silver mining project under moratorium

properties.Ecological/Environmental: Located near the endemic areas where peyote grows
properties.Cultural Elements: Site of New Age tourism and 'Willies' that take tourists to extract peyote
properties.Socio-Political: Many of the land privatizations occurring in the name of peyote conservation are springing from negotiations with ejidatarios in this town and municipality

properties.Ecological/Environmental: Located in Lake Chapala, itself under high environmental alert due to the convergence of contaminants from the Lerma and Santiago rivers
properties.Cultural Elements: A sacred place for the Wixarika and Coca communities; recently also a location of New Age ritual
properties.Socio-Political: Location of a colonial presidio that kept its prisoners in horrific conditions. The presidio was seized by independence insurgents in a battle marking the beginning of Mexico's war of independence

properties.Places and Items: Island of the Scorpion / Isla de los Alacranes
properties.Ecological/Environmental: Located in Lake Chapala, itself under high environmental alert due to the convergence of contaminants from the Lerma and Santiago rivers. The island is now being included in management plans under the Plan de Justicia Wixárika
properties.Cultural Elements: Since the late 20th century, the principal destination of Wixarika pilgrimages to Xapawiyemetá
properties.Socio-Political: Wixarika offerings are mixed with an increased presence of non-Indigenous "pilgrims" offerings. In May 2024, an obelisk was placed by the government to make visible the site as a Wixarika sacred place

properties.Ecological/Environmental: Important ecosystem and site of tourism conflicts; also known for the "jejenes" biting insects
properties.Cultural Elements: Pilgrimage site of the west for all communities, related to Grandmother Ocean. Important for baptisms and connected to the underworld and source of life
properties.Socio-Political: Small tourist hamlet with occasional disputes over encroachment onto Waxiewe, the white island off the shore of San Blas. Recently declared a protected sacred site in Mexico's Plan de Justicia

properties.Ecological/Environmental: In the canyons of the Sierra Madre
properties.Cultural Elements: A sacred site for all communities
properties.Socio-Political: Contentious recently because of intercommunal conflict

properties.Ecological/Environmental: Town founded during the colonial silver mining boom; today a site of religious tourism
properties.Cultural Elements: San Francisco de Asís / Wixarika pilgrimages
